name: Béla Fleck
abstract: Béla Anton Leoš Fleck ( BAY-lə; born July 10, 1958) is an American virtuoso banjo player whose work blends bluegrass with other genres, including jazz, and who is known for his work with the bands New Grass Revival and Béla Fleck and the Flecktones. Fleck has won 18 Grammy Awards and been nominated 41 times.

about_quotes.speaker: Lorraine Feather
about_quotes.evidence: I asked him if he’d play on it, which he did while he was on the road. And it was on my “Ages” album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lorraine Feather interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 22, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Lamb
about_quotes.evidence: my first experience playing with him was at it was called E-town... So we just sat there and just improvised together for about an hour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Lamb interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 7, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: plus you bring in Bela Fleck on banjo... That was in 2006 where you had John Scofield, Bela Fleck, Marc . . . Right, right.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Oliver Nelson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “Come to Indianapolis, you record one or two of my Dad’s tunes... We recorded some tunes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Oliver Nelson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 10, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Monheit
about_quotes.evidence: I worked a lot with Béla Fleck and Mark O’Connor and her early albums which I just loved.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Monheit interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 20, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Sheller
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yes. I know what you’re saying and I remember Bela Fleck, the wonderful banjo player said that he just kept a list of titles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Sheller interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 9, 2022
